candidates.stmt:
My name is Callum Naylor and I will be standing as one of the three Labour Party Candidates for Birkdale at the forthcoming Local Election in May 2026.

Having lived all my life in Birkdale, I know our local community well. I am passionate about continuing to make a positive impact on our area. For example, I am steward of the community Book Box in Bedford Park which was installed as a result of my successful application to Penguin Books. I am a volunteer at Southport Football Club and have been deeply involved in their Community Trust charity. I am truly committed to being a visible and accessible point of contact for all Birkdale Ward residents. I value your voice and input, which have already played a crucial role in shaping my understanding of local priorities. These issues include improving local services,
addressing environmental concerns and further developing community facilities.

For more information, please visit my website:
https://www.callumnaylor.co.uk
or my Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/callumnaylorbirkdale/

I've lived in Waterloo and Crosby for 34 years, raised my family here, and spent the last 15 years campaigning to make our area a better place. I’ve proudly supported the work of Christine and Diane in Blundellsands, Brighton-le-Sands and Crosby — and I’m now standing to represent our community on Sefton Council.

I grew up in a Labour and trade union household, where I learned the importance of fairness, opportunity, and standing up for others. I’ve seen the damage done to our area by years of Conservative cuts, but also the strength of Labour values in action — especially through our local Labour council doing its best to protect vital services.

I’d be honoured to represent Blundellsands — and to be a strong local voice for our community.

Rebecca Hanson taught maths in secondary schools with many challenges before going on to lecture in maths education at MMU and for the OU, work at national level and run her own teacher training business. She is a Fellow of the Chartered College of Teaching.
 
She joined the Lib Dems in response to Michael Gove’s decision to cancel consultation on education policy, worked for three years on national education policy development and now has unique expertise in public sector regulation policy.
 
Rebecca is a County Councillor who played a key role in saving her local maternity services and is now working to sort out the UK’s worst youth mental health crisis. Her book ‘Healthcare Coproduction – A Personal Account’ gives some insight into how she has addressed these issues.
 
Rebecca was a high-profile Lib Dem by-election candidate in 2017 when she became the first parliamentary candidate to live-stream every night – taking questions in real time.
 
Rebecca is also a respected environmental campaigner who proposed and successfully defended Cumbria County County Council’s policy to make the whole of Cumbria (not just the Council) carbon neutral.
 
Rebecca now shares her time between Bootle – Liverpool (where her husband works and her daughter is at a local state primary) and Cockermouth Cumbria, while she completes her term as County Councillor.

Keith Cawdron has lived in what is now Sefton Central constituency for 32 years since moving from London
 
He has worked widely in the local voluntary and public sectors: as Chief Executive of Sefton charity Jospice, a senior manager in the NHS, and a close colleague of David Sheppard when he was Bishop of Liverpool.
 
Keith is very involved in the local community and his current local commitments include being a Governor of St Michael’s School Crosby; a Reader at St Luke’s Anglican Church Crosby; a Trustee of Age Concern Liverpool and Sefton and a Trustee of Pause for Hope (a charity to support anyone affected by cancer). He has served as a non-executive director of Sefton Primary Care Trust, giving him intimate knowledge of the pressures facing our local NHS. He also has first-hand family knowledge of the social care system.
 
Keith is an enthusiastic amateur singer, a patron of the gym at Crosby Leisure Centre and a frequent cyclist. He is married to Maureen who has taught English to Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) in Seaforth, Bootle, Southport and Liverpool. They have three adult children, all of whom were educated in Crosby.

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party
candidates.stmt:
Friends, brothers-and-sisters-in-arms and the politically enlightened,
Do you grow tired of the fearmongering politicians hounding you to vote for them, and their boorish political party? Do you grow exasperated of the same-old same-old being spewed out of the House of Commons on a weekly basis? Are you unable to sexually satisfy your wife? If you answered yes to any of these questions, you’re in luck. We here at the glorious OFFICIAL MONSTER RAVING LOONY PARTY™ have big plans for not only this consistency of Blundellsands, but all of Britain. We are coming together; uniting both the left-wing and right-wing under one banner, and marching forward into the future. We will shut down Blundellsands Station and replace it with a Greggs, open 24/7. We will get rid of both manual AND automatic cars to make way for the return of horse-drawn carriages, solidifying our concern for the environment. Through your vote for us, you will be legally recognised as a KNIGHT OF THE LOONIES and be immediately conscripted to close-quarters combat against a polar bear, with only a water gun to defend yourself. Effective immediately. The sole survivor(s) will be graciously rewarded. Only the strong will survive and the dead will lie unburied.
In a world of boring insanity, vote for our super-sanity.
Standing for Southport, to correct the injustices put upon the town by Bootle controlled Sefton

Originally from Liverpool, Mike is 38, a dad of two, and a practising solicitor who has been based in Ormskirk since 2007. In addition to his business in Ormskirk, Mike is an experienced local political campaigner, serving as a Local Councillor for Southport town centre. As a Councillor, Mike has specialised in campaigning to improve Children's Services. 

Away from politics, Mike is an active fundraiser for local charities. He has previously raised money for the Mencap and Whizzkidz by running the London Marathon and the National Autistic Society by running the Great North Run.

And locally in West Lancs, Mike's law firm have raised over £140,000 for local charities including Ormskirk Civic Hall, Woodlands Animal Sanctuary, Derian Children’s Hospice and Macmillan Cancer Support.  

Mike supports Liverpool FC, enjoys running, golfing at Ormskirk Golf Club and, most importantly, spending time with his wife and two young children.

I’m thrilled to be selected as your candidate in the upcoming Litherland by-election, following the shocking resignation of former Labour councillor, Paul Tweed. Why am I standing as your representative? Because what bothers you, bothers me too. I feel that the Labour-run council and our local Labour Councillors do not look after us or our area. I want to make a difference.

I ask you to vote for positive change for Litherland on 21st November with me, Jack Colbert.

Like you, I love our community as a great place to live and work. However, the Labour-dominated council lacks a convincing vision for improvement, and there have been no positive changes made to Litherland. Just look at the state of Church Road year after year. For over 25 years, three Labour councillors have represented our area and we’ve only seen local problems increase.

I’m invested in the future of our area, which is why I will lead local campaigns to:

    Preserve our vital green spaces     Reduce harmful air pollution     Keep our streets free from fly-tipping, and      Reinstate winter fuel payments

It’s hard to understand why all our Labour councillors voted against calling for the Labour government to reinstate payments for our most vulnerable pensioners in September’s full council motion.

You may have spotted me locally, working as a volunteer for the NHS, Plaza Cinema, and British Legion. I am not just showing up at election time. If I become your councillor, I can do even more for and with you.

I can win this election. This is a contest between me and yet another Labour candidate. Even if you normally vote Labour, I ask you to lend me your vote, as you’ll still have 2 Labour councillors in Litherland. By voting for the Green Party on November 21st, you would be choosing to invest in an alternative vision for Litherland; we need to think big and act boldly. As your representative, I commit to work hard, listen to your perspectives and push for truly transformational change in our area.

I care about our town’s future because I live here and am passionate about making it a better to place to live. I helped represent local people when I worked for John Pugh and I have lived and breathed politics and advocacy in my career. I am now ready to take these skills and fight for a better deal for our town.

Our area has so much potential but we have been failed by a Tory MP who hasn’t properly represented our town in Parliament, and a Labour party in Bootle who time and again put Southport’s interests behind other parts of Sefton. The closed signs on our pier simply illustrate how we have been let down.

Southport used to be known as the jewel of the North West and we need that title back. I am ready to make this my priority by encouraging us to harness our natural advantages and lobbying for things that will spur growth. Making Southport a leader in coastal energy and transforming our transport connections to attract more people to live and work here to revive our local economy are part of my vision for a cleaner, greener and prosperous Southport that we can all feel proud to call home.

Martyn Barber has lived in Crosby all his life and works in the ward. He was previously a Councillor for neighbouring Manor Ward (Crosby, Thornton & Hightown).

He has been active in local politics since he joined the Crosby Conservative Association in 1995. Chairman of Sefton Conservatives on several occasions. You can follow his campaign at www.seftoncentralconservatives.org.uk

Hannah Gee believes that our town is best served by someone who lives in our community and understands our local needs.

Hannah said; “One of the reasons I love living here is because of the access to so much green space. I am really concerned about plans to build through Rimrose Valley. Protecting our green spaces is so important for our environment, health and well being. It should be protected for future generations to enjoy.”

“The slow progress of the redevelopment of Crosby village also frustrates me. The failure of our politicians to provide sufficient leadership to give us a place to shop and socialise is noticeable.”
From Metro Mayor to Bootle MP

This election has no bearing on UK Government. However, it will affect who represents you locally, at Sefton Council.

I have lived in Maghull East (formerly known as Sudell Ward) for the majority of my life. As do my extended family. My children attend schools in the area. I know Maghull inside and out.

I am currently a Town Councillor, sitting on Maghull Town Council as the Leader of the Maghull Community Independents. I am visible in the community, as a volunteer and facilitate some if the local Facebook Community groups.

Community spirit is something that makes Maghull stand out from a lot of other areas of Sefton. With this in mind, our community needs are inherently important to me, and I have seen far too many times, that residents concerns have been either ignored, dismissed, or downplayed by the current representation.

That needs to change...

Our town is expanding at an alarming rate. Many are unable to obtain GP appointments, even more can't register with a dentist. Our roads are crumbling and all we get is weak promises from Sefton Labour. They have historically chosen to allow extensive house building in the area, without investing in the infrastructure it desperately needs.

I know how local systems and processes work. I am a problem solver. I have been a Civil Servant with nearly 20 years service, mainly involved in public services at a national level. I have a proven track record of successfully addressing issues, whilst giving clear communication and transparency throughout to residents. To solve many of the local issues we have, that requires dealing with the root causes where the decisions are made that affect us all. I pledge to be proactive in making sure the money promised to us via S106 Developer Contributions are spent, instead of sitting with the council making no change for the better. Its taking too long. We deserve better.

I will be a strong, unbiased voice for you all. Not being restricted by party politics, I can represent you all f

Hello everyone!

I'm Paul McCord the Lydiate and Maghull Community independents candidate for Maghull East Ward.

I have lived in Lydiate and Maghull since 2009 and actually lived in Maghull East Ward itself so hope to be elected to represent them. I live 2 minutes walk away from Maghull East.

I am also a Parish Councillor for Lydiate Parish Council which is within Maghull West and Lydiate  Ward. Out of the 9 councillors, 7 are community independents.

There are many issues which concern me in the ward which is why I wish to stand to try to help our community. A major issue at the moment is the potential of building houses on farmland adjacent to Lambshear Lane which I am vehemently against. This is in Lydiate but will affect the whole area.

There are also other major issues affecting the locality such as the new build estate next to Poverty Lane, and how the builders so far have not created a spine road which has placed incredible strain on Poverty Lane.

Another is the issues affecting the Poppyfields Estate, especially the builders neglect of the lake.

All these new builds are built on flood plains so may be affected by flooding.

Section 106 money which builders pay the council for the privilege of building on this land should be spent on the area but most of it isn't.

All of these above issues will be addressed by me and other community independent councillors.

A major ongoing issue we are against is the double rating council tax payers in Maghull and Lydiate are paying for park maintenance. This has been going on for 8+ years at at least 100k for Maghull and 40k for Lydiate, per year!

The beauty of being a local independent is that you can fight for the local area and its community without being held back by a national party who wants you to do their bidding.

I am a traditional socialist and trade unionist who believes it is the working class who creates the wealth in this country and therefore be the recipients of that wealth.

I hope that you have enj

Bob Teesdale. Conservative Candidate for Merseyside Police and Crime Commissioner

I served as a Bobby for 30 years retiring as Patrol Inspector, i.e., finishing as I started out – on the streets – working 3 shifts.

I am also a private citizen so I have lots of experience of the other side,  looking to the Police for my family’s and my own safety and wellbeing.

I believe the professionalism of the Merseyside Police is admired round the country but it comes at a price, a tendency to move away from a direct connection to the communities they serve.

So, I am putting, balancing the “re-connecting” with the people of Merseyside, with applying the Forces innate professionalism at the at the heart of my plans for the Force.

 Ensuring that the extra officers gained by the Force from the 20,000 being funded by this Government are spread evenly across the 5 Boroughs.

 Ensuring that community groups who work to benefit us all in so many ways are properly supported including victim Support.             

 Expanding and properly utilising the willingness of our community to help directly, by involving  and properly using the Special Constabulary.

  My priority will always be to work to make Merseyside safer.

  Prepared by Chris Hall on behalf of Bob Teesdale. Both of Southport Conservatives. 37/39 Shakespeare St. Southport PR8 5AB

Green councillors are independent voices, allowed to act for the common good without being constrained by a party 'whip' or dogma. Green Party policy is to end privatisation of our NHS and secure free social care for the elderly, funded by a fair system of taxation where all - including big corporations - contribute their fair share. These tax revenues would also help fund a better living wage, child benefit and removal of tuition fees. It goes without saying that Greens support renewable energy sources such as wind, solar and tidal, and want to phase out global-warming fossil fuels including shale gas from fracking. The UN has made clear we have a limited window of opportunity before 2030 to limit climate breakdown; with the Government in Westminster failing to take the action needed, Green Party councillors are stepping up across the UK to take action locally. Green councillors will change the culture of how we do local politics, promoting equality and justice, supporting the most vulnerable in our communities, working to save heritage, libraries, shops and pubs, putting green space and public access above developer profit, and making our streets cleaner and safer for the public.

Neil Doolin was elected as the Green Party's first ever councillor on Sefton Council in May's 2024 local elections, beating the sitting Labour Party councillor and Cabinet member by 1412 to 1307 votes. Neil has been a local campaigner for the people of Waterloo & Seaforth for several years working on issues such as cleaner air for residents, supporting the campaign against the plan to build a dual carriageway through Rimrose Valley and successfully preventing Sefton Council's plan to demolish the community library in Waterloo.   Neil has worked in the NHS since 1990 as a nursing assistant and then qualified as a registered mental health nurse in 1994. He has worked in a variety of clinical areas supporting service users and families including dementia services, a homeless outreach team in Liverpool and in recent years as an operational manager for community mental health teams across south sefton. Neil has a keen interest in developing healthier communities and understands the link between climate, poverty and ill health.   Neil and his wife Lyndsey have lived in Sefton for the past 10 years.

I am proud to be standing for Waterloo because this is a community worth fighting for.
Like many residents, I care deeply about our streets, our shared spaces and the people who live here. Waterloo is a great place to live, and I want to work to make it even better.
one of my key priorities is tackling fly-tipping and litter.
Too many areas are blighted by waste that harms our environment and drags down community pride. I will work with residents to keep Waterloo clean.
I also want to make sure help reaches those who need it most.
Whether it's support with the cost of living, access to services, or simply having a councillor who listens and acts, I will always stand up for our most vulnerable residents.
Together, we can build a cleaner, fairer and stronger Waterloo.

**Remember people, actions speak louder than words, and I would advise you to look at my social media posts where I have documented my work in the ward so far, clearing fly tipping, supporting charities, helping residents and getting things done**

**It’s time to elect someone who’s not afraid to speak out, roll up their sleeves and persevere until they get things done, and that’s exactly what I will do.**
